title: Tests are change detectors
hook: A test is not there to prove you are smart. It is there to notice when future you accidentally changes the deal.

blocks.html: A suite can cover lines and still miss behavior. The useful question is not how much code ran, but what user-facing promise would break if this test failed.

blocks.title: zsh · focused test
blocks.caption: The useful signal is usually already there.

lines.t: PASS keeps tax when coupon changes subtotal

blocks.html: Write tests around the contract: inputs, outputs, state transitions, and error paths. Implementation details deserve fewer tests because they should be allowed to move.

blocks.caption: The assertion names behavior a user would notice.

blocks.html: Fast unit tests catch most logic changes. A smaller number of integration and end-to-end tests catches wiring. Too much of either creates blind spots or slow feedback.

items.label: behavior per test is easiest to debug
tests that only prove mocks work

beats.heading: A failing test is a useful alarm

blocks.html: Good tests make change safer because they remember the promises the code made before your brain got busy.
